name: WOOVO Phuket Patong – by Swandor
detail: Good located in a side street at patong, only a 10 minutes walk to the beach. Nice view to the ocean from the 8th floor. The rooms were big and clean, modern and new. Would really recommend this hotel.

name: Crest Resort & Pool Villas
detail: When we drove up to this resort, I was thinking wow this is an amazing property, wish it's where we are staying. Then I saw the sign and we all got excited. It has some serious curb appeal. We drove up, parked our car and saw a lot of construction still going on. Security called for 2 golf carts to come down and pick up our luggage and the 3 of us. We took a steep and dangerous ride, passengers in the back need to hang on for dear life. The reception desk is located outdoors under some of the architecture. A pool wraps around the front of some of the pool access rooms. It's beautiful, but in an Ikea sort of way. Our room, the sea view room, while somewhat stylish looks great with a wonderful view but that's just the surface. If you stay in the room, you will find that there's only about 2 outlets inside and 1 outlet on the balcony. The wifi signal was a bit weak but I speedtested it and it measured in at 5Mb up and down. The finishes in the room are pretty low quality but I've found that with a lot of Phuket hotels. The restroom facilities are bad as they give you pretty much no privacy. I wouldn't recommend it for a family stay. With only 2 outlets, you have to plug in the hair dryer outside of the restroom area. The doors are glass and frosted in some parts but lend very little privacy. The toilet room is separated from the shower but the door only goes part way up letting every little noise you make out into the living space. Water pressure was low on the rainfall showerhead. The bathroom counter hardly had any usable space, you better learn to live tight. Beds are super firm. There wasn't much consideration with regard to safety so you better watch your kids. The ride up to the property was dangerous as is the property itself. Every doorway has a ledge so the moment you step out, you are falling down and you can really hurt yourself if you're not careful. The pool is amazing, views of 2 beaches and it has a bar area. Some rooms will have pool access at thei

name: Niranapa Boutique Resort
detail: The restaurant served very good food with a lot of options.

name: Lub d Phuket Patong
detail: This is one of the best hostels, more like a hotel to me, that I stayed at in Thailand. I reserved the smallest private room and I was extremely happy with it: it was super clean, modern and functional. Everything worked 100%. The bathroom was clean and immaculate. Water temperature and pressure was excellent. The staff was friendly and excellent as well. I highly suggest that you utilize their inhouse travel services (STA Travel). Toni from STA, booked a full day island tour that was affordable and luxurious. That's all I'm going to say. If you can opt for a better package, go with it, you won't regret it. Also worth mentioning, is that there are so many activities within the hostel that can allow you to relax, socialize and network with people. Somedays, you can just stay inside the 'hostel', away from the hustle of Phuket within a nice environment and still enjoy yourself. Location is excellent: next to a lot of restaurants, food stalls, massage parlor and Walking ST.

name: Saba Suites Hotel Patong Phuket
detail: Location is nearest to walkiing street just 2mins aways.

name: Nai Yang Beach Resort & Spa
detail: For an affordable/ budget hotel, this is highly recommended.

name: PeranaKan Boutique Hotel
detail: I loved the property! It's in New Phuket town, so if your trying to party all night, maybe stay closer to Patong. But if you like a great nights rest, trustworthy staff, and a safe location OYO 130 is the perfect place for a solo traveler. Toni, Waen, and Moi Young their dog make sure of that. I had 2 fresh water bottles eveyday, folded my clothes when I left them out. Even forgot to lock up my valuables a few times and found nicely organized on my bed. When it came time to checkout, they were even nice enough to extend my scooter rental for a few hours at an adjusted rate so I could see more of Phuket before my flight. Highly recommend for any style traveler.

name: Dewa Phuket Resort & Villas
detail: i booked a regular suite at the dewa phuket hotel. i arranged for airport pick up and was greeted by the driver upon my arrival. i had requested for an early check in and my request was accommodated. even better, they had upgraded me to a private pool villa! the private pool villa was awesome -- own pool, living room, big, comfy princess bed, outdoor bathroom, etc. when i had lunch at the terrace grill (the resort's restaurant), i noticed they didn't serve chicken green thai curry, and i mentioned to this to the waiter. he immediately said he will request the kitchen to prepare it specially for me. so good! the only thing i found weird abut dewa phuket hotel was when i took the laundry bag with me, since i had used it to store my dirty clothes. i do this in all other hotels i have stayed at (take the laundry bag, whether it was plastic or cloth, to store dirty clothes). upon check out, they asked me to return it.

name: Naiyang Park Resort
detail: We loved the stay and the hotel staff was super helpful.

name: The Bell Airport Phuket Hotel
detail: We were stranded in Phuket after Air Asia cancelled our return flights without giving other options to fly back home, due to Malaysian government directives on Covid-19 MRO. Booked the hotel on the same day we checked in. Staff was very helpful, even helped to arrange car rental for our extended stay. Room is very spacious, adequate facilities for the price we paid. Kids just enjoyed playing and swimming in the pool. Halal eateries and convenience store within walking distance. With free Airport shuttle, it us very convenient if you arrive late or need to fly early
